title: <cstdio> should be avoided
description: "<cstdio>" should not be usedAnalyze your codeconsistency-conventionalmaintainabilityCode Smellbased-on-misralock-inWhy is this an issue?More InfoThis includes file and I/O functionsfgetpos,fopen,ftell,gets,perror,remove,rename, etc.Streams and file I/O have a large number of unspecified, undefined and implementation-defined behaviors associated with them.Noncompliant code example#include <cstdio> /* Noncompliant */ void fn() { char_t array[10]; gets(array); /* Can lead to buffer over-run */ }Available In:Catch issues on the fly,in your IDEDetect issues in your GitHub, Azure DevOps Services, Bitbucket Cloud, GitLab repositoriesAnalyze code in youron-premise CIDeveloper EditionAvailable Since9.1©2025SonarSource Sàrl. All rights reserved.Privacy Policy|Cookie Policy|Terms of Use
recommendation: Remove the problematic code and refactor as needed.

checks.message: Remove the problematic code and refactor as needed.
Replace C stdio functions with C++ stream I/O functions
